feedback.natural: Dejar de funcionar is the standard phrase for devices that stop working.
feedback.alternatives: Trabajar is an English transfer here. Servir can mean to be useful, but not the clean product-failure phrase.
feedback.chunk: Ha dejado de funcionar is essential for complaints and customer service.

feedback.natural: Hay un error con mi reserva is polite and clear. Con vistas al mar is the fixed hotel phrase.
feedback.alternatives: Conflicto sounds too strong. Mirada means a look, not a view from a room.
feedback.chunk: Creo que hay un error con mi reserva is a useful complaint opener.

best: Me da la impresión de que

feedback.natural: Me da la impresión de que softens an opinion and sounds diplomatic.
feedback.alternatives: Me cae bien is for liking people, me hace falta expresses need, and me toca means it is my turn.
feedback.chunk: Me da la impresión de que... is useful when you want to disagree carefully.

feedback.natural: Tener en cuenta means to take something into account and is extremely common.
feedback.alternatives: Hacer en cuenta and dar en cuenta are not natural. Tomar en cuenta also works in many varieties.
feedback.chunk: Tener en cuenta + noun is useful in academic and work planning.

feedback.natural: Ponernos de acuerdo means to agree or reach an agreement together.
feedback.alternatives: Hacernos de acuerdo is not natural. Estar de acuerdo describes agreement, but here the process of agreeing is better.
feedback.chunk: Ponerse de acuerdo is perfect for scheduling and group decisions.

feedback.natural: Llevar a cabo means to carry out or execute a plan.
feedback.alternatives: Llevar encima means to carry on you, llevarse bien means to get along, and llevar por delante has a different meaning.
feedback.chunk: Llevar a cabo un plan/proyecto/iniciativa is a strong professional collocation.

feedback.natural: Merece la pena means it is worth it and sounds natural in recommendations.
feedback.alternatives: Tiene la pena is wrong. Vale la pena also works, especially in many varieties.
feedback.chunk: Merece la pena + infinitive is high-frequency for advice.

feedback.natural: A raíz de means as a result of or following an event.
feedback.alternatives: En raíz de and con raíz de are not fixed expressions. Por alone would require a different structure.
feedback.chunk: A raíz de + noun is useful in news, reports, and explanations.

feedback.natural: Te vendría bien is a natural way to ask if a time works for someone.
feedback.alternatives: Caería, saldría, and pasaría do not fit this scheduling phrase.
feedback.chunk: ¿Te vendría bien...? is polite and useful for meetings and plans.

feedback.natural: Quedarse sin means to run out of something.
feedback.alternatives: The other prepositions do not form the expression for running out.
feedback.chunk: Me he quedado sin... is useful for money, battery, time, food, and supplies.

feedback.natural: Plantear una duda is a formal and natural academic collocation.
feedback.alternatives: Levantar una duda is not the normal collocation. The other verbs do not fit the meaning.
feedback.chunk: Plantear una duda/un problema/una cuestión is useful in class and meetings.

feedback.natural: Hacer hincapié en means to emphasize and is common in presentations.
feedback.alternatives: Hacer énfasis en can work, but hacer hincapié en is the stronger fixed phrase here.
feedback.chunk: Hacer hincapié en + noun is useful in academic and professional speaking.

feedback.natural: Al fin y al cabo means after all and introduces a final justification.
feedback.alternatives: The phrase is fixed with al; the other prepositions break it.
feedback.chunk: Al fin y al cabo... is common when giving perspective or softening a point.

feedback.natural: Estar de acuerdo con is the correct verb-preposition pattern.
feedback.alternatives: The other prepositions are common in other structures, but not after estar de acuerdo.
feedback.chunk: Estoy de acuerdo con... is a high-frequency discussion phrase.

feedback.natural: No me queda claro means it is not clear to me and sounds natural.
feedback.alternatives: No me deja claro would need a subject that explains something. No me sale claro is not natural.
feedback.chunk: No me queda claro... is useful when asking for clarification politely.

feedback.natural: Tomar medidas means to take measures and is common in formal problem-solving.
feedback.alternatives: Tomar acciones is understandable but less idiomatic in Spain. Pasos can work in other contexts but not as fixed here.
feedback.chunk: Tomar medidas is useful for problems, risks, and complaints.

feedback.natural: En cuanto a introduces or shifts to a topic: as for / regarding.
feedback.alternatives: Debido a gives a cause, a pesar de gives contrast, and con tal de gives purpose/condition.
feedback.chunk: En cuanto a... is useful for emails, reports, and meetings.

feedback.natural: Dar por hecho means to take for granted or assume.
feedback.alternatives: The other participles do not form this expression.
feedback.chunk: Di por hecho que... is useful when explaining a mistaken assumption.

feedback.natural: Lo antes posible means as soon as possible.
feedback.alternatives: The phrase is fixed with lo; the other articles do not work.
feedback.chunk: Lo antes posible is a practical phrase for requests and deadlines.

feedback.natural: Por si acaso means just in case and expresses precaution.
feedback.alternatives: Por lo tanto gives a result, a cambio de means in exchange for, and en lugar de means instead of.
feedback.chunk: Por si acaso is very common in everyday planning.

feedback.natural: Dejar de funcionar is the standard phrase for devices that stop working.
feedback.alternatives: Trabajar is an English transfer here. Servir can mean to be useful, but not the clean product-failure phrase.
feedback.chunk: Ha dejado de funcionar is essential for complaints and customer service.

feedback.natural: Hay un error con mi reserva is polite and clear. Con vistas al mar is the fixed hotel phrase.
feedback.alternatives: Conflicto sounds too strong. Mirada means a look, not a view from a room.
feedback.chunk: Creo que hay un error con mi reserva is a useful complaint opener.
